findings.summary: the doc's load-bearing sentence quoted a DIFFERENT run than it shipped — '~17 s apart, the second serving the retry' vs 516 ms and RETRY_RECONNECTED 'not-attempted' in the committed record; the figure was real but from an earlier run, narrated from memory. Doc rewritten from the records with a standing instruction not to restate figures from memory

the kill record was not produced by the committed script (missing the mode key), and RECURRED one commit later (missing sampleNames/mcpServers/eventKeys) while asserting recordVersion 2 — a false provenance claim on the same file. Kill re-run; both records now carry identical shapes

2 of RFC-035 §0's 3 required outputs were missing: the retry was conditional so never fired in kill mode (now unconditional, with its own sentinel, and it landed); 'whether kb_* tools remain listed' proved NOT observable from the transcript at all (init enumerates only the 29 built-ins; the sole MCP signal is mcp_servers[].status='pending') — recorded as a gap in the observation surface, reviewer independently confirmed no other event carries the list

'Claude Code transparently respawns a crashed server' was inferred from a second n:1 in the log, indistinguishable from a second pre-existing instance; the wrapper now logs a startup event with its pid, and the re-run shows 3 distinct spawns with 3 distinct pids each strictly after the prior exit — reviewer verified no third explanation survives
the record's method string claimed SIGKILL-after-first-call in BOTH modes, contradicting the hang column's whole point (the wrapper stays alive holding the pipe); now mode-aware
'no error surfaced' in hang mode is bounded by the probe's own 300s timeout, not a contract; now qualified in the findings table rather than only in the limits section
respawn was inferred from a second DEATH, so a respawn serving no further call read as false; now counted from startup events
sandbox cleanup was not in a finally; the sandbox holds a real OAuth credential and an exception path must not leave it in /tmp
the hang row said the retry 'never returned', implying a third call was made and hung; kbToolCallsAttempted is 2 — it was never ISSUED, the turn wedged on call #2
the agentObservationUnverified flag fires only at zero kb results, so a PARTIAL harvest raises no flag; mitigated because the hang conclusion rests on the brain file, independent of the parser
the wrapper's death trigger assumes the next stdout chunk belongs to the Nth request; safe under serial request/response, which the reviewer exercised directly against a raw SDK client in both modes
no dedupe by tool_use_id, so a result echoed in two events would double-count; unexercised risk (3 uses / 3 results observed)
